# Vendored man (groff_man) specification

`spec.txt` is the `groff_man(7)` manual page — the specification of the `man` macro package that
defines the man page markup this direction reads. It is the version shipped with
[GNU roff (groff)](https://www.gnu.org/software/groff/) `1.24.1` (see `VERSION`), rendered to plain
text from its public HTML edition at
<https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/groff_man.7.html> (manual page dated 2026-03-14).

The page is authored by the Free Software Foundation, Ingo Schwarze, and G. Branden Robinson, and
released under a permission notice that grants the right to make and distribute verbatim copies (see
`LICENSE`). It is vendored here as a public format specification — an allowed source of truth for
this clean-room implementation — to document the macro and escape surface the reader parses.

Only the prose **specification** is used: the macros, escape sequences, registers, and structural
conventions it describes drive the reader. Any expected conversion output is regenerated by the
pinned oracle binary, never taken from this document.

`spec.txt` reproduces the manual's sections (NAME through SEE ALSO) as rendered text, omitting the
HTML edition's site COLOPHON. The `LICENSE` permission notice is transcribed from the manual's
source comment header (the HTML edition does not reprint it).

Refresh or re-pin with `tools/fetch-man-spec.sh` (edit `GROFF_VERSION` to bump).
